title: Gentle Braised Venezuelan Tomato Stew
description: A rich and nostalgic Venezuelan dish featuring datterini tomatoes, this stew is a diabetic friendly option for a comforting meal. It is made with tender loving care to bring out the natural sweetness of the tomatoes.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground cumin and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the datterini tomatoes, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, until the stew has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
